name: Yuanbao Jiaozi
review: Our favorite dumplings in SF. Baby especially likes the fish and pepper ones. Small spot, quiet during weekday lunch but can get crowded at peak hours.

name: My Father’s Kitchen
review: High chairs too big for 9 month old and he almost slipped out through the leg opening. Was able to eat pieces of brisket from the pho, and he LOVED the coconut chicken.

name: Dumpling Home
review: Family-friendly seating. Gave baby his own water straw cup / plastic utensils. Dumplings are excellent though a bit saltier than Yuanbao Jiaozi. Veggie bao also BLW-friendly and low sodium.

name: Ebisu
review: Baby had pieces of tofu from the miso soup, tamago/shrimp from chirashi bowl, and flakes of miso cod (his favorite ever). Did not check for high chairs as baby was too young at the time.

name: Bella Trattoria
review: High chairs a bit too big for 9-month old but seemed safe. Menu is extensive but not great for BLW. Gnocchi was quite salty and veggies were not quite soft enough. Baby loved the free bread.

name: Harbin Hot Springs
review: Inglesina clips to their outdoor picnic tables. All their food seems to be low sodium. Baby loved their tofu and broccoli sides. They even made him a baby 1-egg omelet for breakfast!
